title: podman run: conflict between --pod and --network
body: ### Issue Description When I run podman run command and add the argument --pod the published ports is correctly added to image but using --network argument prevents the published ports from working. ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Create a network: `podman network create n1` 2. Create a pod and publish a port: `podman pod create --name p1 --network n1 -p5000:5000` 3. Run and add a container to the pod: `podman run --pod p1 -d quay.io/podman/hello:latest` 4. Run another container (same image) but add the network agument: `podman run --pod p1 --network n1 -d quay.io/podman/hello:latest` ### Describe the results you received I noticed that the first container will have the pod ports but the second one is not. Running `podman ps -a` returns the following (notice the port column): ### Describe the results you expected Not sure if it makes sense to use network argument along the pod one but we should either have an error/warning or the port should be added successfully. ### podman info output ```yaml host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.42.0 cgroupControllers: - cpu - io - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.13-2.fc43.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.13, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 98.09 systemPercent: 0.48 userPercent: 1.43 cpus: 16 databaseBackend: sqlite distribution: distribution: fedora variant: workstation version: "43" emulatedArchitectures: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 524288 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 524288 size: 65536 kernel: 6.17.8-300.fc43.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 23191212032 memTotal: 33344786432 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: aardvark-dns-1.17.0-1.fc43.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.17.0 package: netavark-1.17.0-1.fc43.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.17.0 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.25-1.fc43.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.25 commit: d9a0adce065c7747ab88ea6ccc42b15a626e08e1 rundir: /run/user/1000/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +LIBKRUN +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: /usr/bin/pasta package: passt-0^20250919.g623dbf6-1.fc43.x86_64 version: | pasta 0^20250919.g623dbf6-1.fc43.x86_64 Copyright Red Hat GNU General Public License, version 2 or later This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it. comments.body:
This is expected, see the docs https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#network-mode-net

> If used together with --pod, the container does not join the pod’s network namespace.

By setting this option you ask for a new namespace on the same network so the container doesn't join the pods (well infra container really) network namespace
